title: “No act of kindness, no matter how small, is ever wasted.“
caption:
“No act of kindness, no matter how small, is ever wasted.“ 

Got to spend a chunk of the semester helping with an amazing vision from a friend. What started with me coming to do a two day talk turned into hours every week and it was totally worth it 🙏🏾 

One of the issues I’ve realized both with schools and even as adults is that the wrong questions get asked. Some students got chewed out for not doing work but who is really about to admit it’s because of not having resources (like computers). I know people that refuse to ask for directions when lost, let alone more serious things 😅 

With that I figured why not get laptops not just a few students but for some who want to start online classes, and have been doing great in classes too. Got a mix of teacher recommendations for those kids that needed access for a variety of reasons. Even had some laptops to put into a new student center being built and for counselors. 

Special special thank you to all the friends who gave to make it possible ❤️ It’s pretty amazing to see how easy it is to help impact more because others want the same thing as us..better opportunities for the kids coming after up 🙏🏾

I think I want a challenge to see how fast we could do 1,000 scholarships in the US and abroad 🤔 I’m not gonna be like the directionless person not asking for help I definitely will need plenty but it’ll be fun to see more happy students getting to go after their dreams 😇

#atlanta #education #giveback #forthekids

title: Kids’ clothes and shoes matter.
caption:
Kids’ clothes and shoes matter.
We’re partnering with Global Abiding Hope Inc., led by Naomi Karaya, to create meaningful impact in Kenyan schools.

For many children, this is their first pair of shoes and it changes everything. Dignity, Confidence, Hope, and Gifts are treasured for a lifetime.

Please DM us if you have children’s clothes or shoes to donate. This is only the beginning of our work in Kenya. Stay tuned.

Naomi reminds me so much of my grandmother, Helen. I truly appreciated this interview, and we are honored and excited to be part of this journey of change.

caption:
Wednesday’s are senior days and I spotted a cute old lady standing with a full buggy as I was about to walk inside. I asked “Could I help you load up your car?” She said “Thank God, I was just asking where a man might be because this is for my friend and I know we get tired just shopping 😭” Once she saw I was gonna make sure her friend was good, she sped off 😂🏎️💨

Summer of 10th grade this was my first official non-hustle job as a courtesy clerk. Pushing buggies in the heat is not something I’d love to do now….but helping people always will be ❤️

caption:
Creating whatever we want starts with today 😎 I’ll probably do some longer versions of these on YT because cramming this into 90 second was tough 😂 

Before talking initially, I’ll point out I researched and offered help with things I could spot he would need but likely wasn’t best use of time. Then after our first convo died, I just followed up and on our call, He was interested in actually meeting. He even shared the spreadsheets he used to analyze companies to make his offers make sense. 

People spend thousands to chat with people for an hour…Wouldn’t buying a flight (with points), a hotel (with a discount from a friend) and buying some food to sit down and learn from people who are ahead of you in the game be a no brainer?

caption:
We never know what we’ll get out of life if we don’t ask 🗣️

The question “Can you help me get a free paddle?” (He knows I’ve gotten free stuff for content)

Response: “Sure 😎 you come up with 2 video ideas and I’ll bring two. Be as creative as you want no right or wrong I’ll bring all the equipment then I’ll give you one of the paddles I got for doing the same thing.” 

It was fun and refreshing to work on this. Asking questions is a cheat code in life if you ask the right things and aren’t afraid of hearing no until you get it. 

Thanks for the reminder that the kids coming up are pretty awesome @atl.stone1

title: Gratitude, Growth & The Universal Laws of Abundance ✨
caption:
Gratitude, Growth & The Universal Laws of Abundance ✨

Everything starts with mindset—the way we think, feel, and believe shapes what we experience. At The Viberary, we facilitate experiences that help people connect and reflect more deeply with themselves and others, uncovering the beliefs that shape their reality. This month, we’re diving into abundance over anxiety and exploring how to shift our relationship with money.

We sat down with our special guest @phillip.karaya who shared insights that align with the Universal Laws:

1. The Law of Correspondence – Your external world mirrors your internal beliefs. Abundance starts within.

2. The Law of Compensation – What you appreciate, appreciates. Gratitude opens the door for more.

3. The Law of Divine Oneness – Everything is connected—your thoughts, actions, and emotions shape your reality.

This Friday, we’re bringing these principles to life with @blackcoffeeatlanta & @FinanceWithPhil for a transformative experience on money, mindset, and abundance. See you there! 🔑💸

One of my favorite moments was one of the guys saying “this feels like therapy” and it’s wonderful that no matter where you go in the world we all can enjoy learning and listening from all walks of life 😎 

Most of us knew each other less than a week but it would be hard to tell 😅I’m a strong believer in relationships are measured by depth not time and this was some quality time with strangers made friends. This is also a reminder that I’m so blessed for the people in my life because they’ve made these moments happen often but it never makes it harder to cherish meeting genuine people.

title: In the last Week I got to help two investors launch Airbnbs in Atlanta and Houston 🔥 I’ll list out the different essenti
caption:
In the last Week I got to help two investors launch Airbnbs in Atlanta and Houston 🔥 I’ll list out the different essentials and some other cheats below 😎👇🏾

-Smart Locks(for security reasons a key that could be easily copied shouldn’t be used…folks are crazy and you never know so make it difficult for folks to sneak in your property 🏡)

-Ring (Not sponsored so use anyone 😂 but have a way to see how many folks enter the property in the case of a party or a handyman you have to let in while you are snowboarding in Utah only to find out someone put a wig down the drain…true story 😂)

-Automated Messages for Guest Experiences(only have to do it once and they normally don’t have to change besides updating the smart lock codes in between guests and some locks will actually randomize and send to guests for you 💪🏾)

This could be a much longer lists but to my other hosts, what other tips do you find essential 😎

You can do 25 referrals a year so no reason to not help more people do something they want to do and it won’t cost them a thing 😎

title: Here’s how to become a Superhost on Airbnb! 🏡
caption:
Here’s how to become a Superhost on Airbnb! 🏡 

Every quarter, Airbnb evaluates your hosting performance over the past 12 months for all listings on your account. (However, you didn’t have to host for the entire 12 months to qualify.)

1. Completed at least 10 trips or 3 reservations that total at least 100 nights

2. Maintain a 90% response rate or higher

3. Maintain a less than 1% cancellation rate, with exceptions made for those that fall under the Extenuating Circumstances policy 😉 

4. Maintain a 4.8 overall rating (A review counts towards Superhost status when either both the guest and the Host have submitted a review, or the 14-day window for reviews is over, whichever comes first).

If you meet the program requirements above by the assessment date, you’ll automatically become a Superhost— you don’t need to apply. They’ll notify you of your status at the end of each assessment period. Keep in mind it may take up to 1 week for your Superhost badge to show up.

title: "There's a marked difference between acquaintances and friends. Most people really don't become friends. They become dee
caption:
"There's a marked difference between acquaintances and friends. Most people really don't become friends. They become deep and serious acquaintances. But in a friendship you get to know the spirit of another person; and your values coincide." 

Once again @codycaswell_ & @seanade helped curate days of awesome people who share intentionality on how we use our gifts in life and who we build with. 

To all of the fellas 😎, was elated to see those I know and honored to begin building authentic friendships for all who we just crossed life paths. 

One of the most beautiful things about this group is that even though there’s collectively more assets controlled by the group than Kiribati’s GDP all come to the same consensus that the question to answer internally is “How can I live the most fulfilling life?” while everyone else through action and challenges embodies “How can I help?”

I shared one of my struggles in this phase of life and business of how I can be torn on where allocate my time and mental energy which @codycaswell_ pointed out in our 5+ hours life deep dive with @edwin.byler & @nickmaciag “You struggle because you have the mind of a CEO and a heart of service.” and “You should probably stop doing anything eating less than 5 hours of time and find the right partner to find more time freedom….and not go replace it.” (guilty on many occasions 😅) I could do a 2 hour plus recap but to conclude, I feel refreshed, gained clarity, got challenged and am ready to keep going 🚀

title: Besides the amazing times with friends and discusssing business was hands down the “Pen Game”
caption:
Besides the amazing times with friends and discusssing business was hands down the “Pen Game”

Everyone starts with the same item and you spend a set time (1 hour when we did it because I had a flight to catch) and you see what you can trade up too without spending any money or telling anyone you’re playing a game. It was challenging but as the game progresses and post recap it’s all about perception economics. Plenty of people will give you things for free just because but you’ve gotta understand what it is they want and the many many things we can use outside of money to exchange value. I did see a guy rob target in the midst of it and sell tvs for $30-$100 and even though Target says it’s $300 to that guy possibly getting sent to jail and $30 was worth that 32 inch…it’s not always that extreme but it’s definitely worth thinking about 🤔

title: Happy Birthday to MY AMAZING MOM ❤️
caption:
Happy Birthday to MY AMAZING MOM ❤️

Watching, hearing and feeling the way you’ve positively impacted people through teaching, community engagement, being a shoulder to cry on, someone willing to pray for others, someone willing to give to others has sparked me to push to do as much as I can to do good in life.

People love you and they get to partially experience you while I got to feel all the love, support and guidance with you and Pops hand in hand raising a family, tackling a career and having fun along the way. 

You constantly remind me not to kill myself doing a 1000 things yet I still don’t think I could manage doing a fraction of what I’ve seen you accomplish. I’ll always be cheering for you and helping however I can. You deserve so much more than flowers and even though you denied my birthday gift to you because you told me “my fulfillment as I age comes from helping others more than helping and just enjoying myself.” I’m honoring that can’t wait to help you push another educational project overseas reach the finish line ❤️

Happy Birthday Dr. Mommy couldn’t have been more blessed to have you with us another year. Enjoy being 35 it only comes around once 😘

title: The saddest thing is that people take advantage of those who are genuinely interested in learning and growing in fields
caption:
The saddest thing is that people take advantage of those who are genuinely interested in learning and growing in fields they aren’t strong in (YET)

We see a lot of Gurus talking in circles and setting people up to fail. We all get scammed one way or another as we learn but if we keep getting scammed, there’s a larger issue at hand. 

Often it’s not what you know, it’s who you know that can help shortcut that learning process! If you haven’t done it yet you have time but not money. If getting started in business you probably have neither 😂 But once you get a bit past the beginning you may have both but the difference is you care a lot about how you spend time. If you want the time of someone you can’t afford to pay, figure out how to free up their time and add them value and you’ll definitely get their time for free 😎 If you pitched them and they don’t want it try again with someone else because eventually someone will say yes 👍🏾

title: Today marks 4 Years since I legally started my first business 😎🎉🎉
caption:
Today marks 4 Years since I legally started my first business 😎🎉🎉

In TRUE business fashion the day had plenty of curveballs 😭 

I woke up thinking I’ll just pack and get some work done it’ll be a easy enough day…😂😂😂😂😂

My cleaners for @turo all had last minute emergencies and I knew I needed to add some diesel exhaust fluid to one car that got delayed until 3. Never afraid to roll up my sleeves went to go clean up the cars, add the fluid, drop off new door handles at another rental then go grab dry cleaning then head to the airport. 

Got to the airport about an hour and a half (they stop taking check in luggage an hour prior so I was in good shape even with a large line). I get to the front and the woman tells me first “You have to have a printed covid card”……We talk I ask nicely if I login to Walgreens Portal can that be accepted so you know it’s not fake? 🥺

Sureeee… BET! *internet moves slow 😨 andddd I know I’ve got like 10 minutes”…BUT I GET IT! 😜

Thennnnnn…🫣
“You have to have a booster or Doctors Note to get on this flight because you got your vaccine over 270 days ago…so you’ll just have to look for another flight cause you can’t make it there/back and it’s pretty booked for a few days….”

My face 😐 so of course I kindly ask “What if I can get a Doctors note??”

…..”How are you going to get one in a few minutes….” 📲

Andddd within a few minutes I had it and made it on the flight. 

Moral of the Story: A positive mental attitude can get stuff done even when it all looks like it’s going to 💩.

Be kind to people, the woman did not have to give me those extra few minutes but since I was nice to her she extended some grace to me ❤️

Appreciate the people you have in your life and they’ll appreciate you. In 4 years a lot has changed but funny enough the message that started my day and that just saved my behind have been in my corner since before this crazy 4 year journey got started 💪🏾❤️

You can’t control anything but yourself(1000x easier said than done but) and remember….Everything in life is negotiable so be mindful how you negotiate with others and especially yourself 😇

Last slide shows how entrepreneurship affects you 😂

title: For a long time I couldn’t float and even didn’t care to try to learn because of a fear of drowning(almost drown twice a
caption:
For a long time I couldn’t float and even didn’t care to try to learn because of a fear of drowning(almost drown twice as a kid 😅) 

Sometimes I think life is a little like trying to float. It doesn’t work unless you learn how to relax, breathe and position yourself 😌

Don’t be so afraid of drowning in whatever it is going on that you forget to relax and put yourself in a place to be at peace with whatever is going on. 

Every place I travel with water I’ll happily spend as much time as possible floating and just enjoying being. There’s a lot of beauty in relaxation and understanding when to let go 😇 

There’s always going to be some chaos no matter what you do just remember that you beat all the hardest things you’ve ever dealt with up until this point, why not keep that same energy. 🧘🏾‍♂️

I prefer to be at peace and realize it’s a choice to let the things around me not get me off center because at the end of the day all you can control is you. The sooner you get that the simpler accepting things outside of that scope stops bothering you. Doesn’t me they never will but once in a blue moon beats once an hour 🙌🏾

title: "Coming together is a beginning, staying together is progress, and working together is success." 🙌🏾
caption:
"Coming together is a beginning, staying together is progress, and working together is success." 🙌🏾

A conversation at a cigar lounge turned into a phone call which turned into buying a half a million dollar crib a lot faster than the original timeline.(about a month early actually 🔥) Plenty of great hands in the deal but more importantly than money is doing business and life with the right people. ✊🏾

We are WAYYYYYY stronger together than apart. 💪🏾💪🏾💪🏾

STOP 🛑  thinking you’ve got to do it all alone. If you have the humility to say where you are and what you want and share it with others, SYNERGY brings it all together. 

I talk a lot of group economics once you figure out how to set a vision, execute and work with others there’s practically nothing that can’t come to life. 

Look out for what will be a fun launch party coming up for a dope Airbnb concept coming up this month 😎

title: Leverage is 🔑 to scaling but not more than anything you can handle or have experience handling.
caption:
Leverage is 🔑 to scaling but not more than anything you can handle or have experience handling.

Whether you are looking for investors or use banks (credit) as investors plays devils advocate. If someone came to YOU with no experience managing 10% of what they are asking you for would you invest that money with confidence ? 🤷🏾‍♂️

A great secret is to find the people to partner with to add your experiences together! A strong team beats a strong individual all day. 💪🏾

Everyone starts somewhere so if you decide to leverage money with no reserves that’s your business just understand your risks. Move with focus, intentionality & as SOON as possible find counsel from someone who has been where you are or is stronger in that area. Best way to get them to care to help you is to find how to help them. 🤝

One of the most powerful questions that’s super underrated is “How can I help?” 

Great question! 👍🏾

title: “None of us is as smart as all of us.” 💡
caption:
“None of us is as smart as all of us.” 💡 

Go and find your tribe and partner up to get it done. I see a lot of people talk about wanting to get into business but can’t because of capital or understanding. Grab some friends with passion, business acumen(or willing to gain some) and resources and the sky is the limit. 

Raising my first $100k of outside money took about a year. Took about 2 weeks with some of my business partners now. I’m sure it’ll get to be one phone call pretty soon 😅

People initially invest because of who you are and eventually because of what you do. If no one wants to invest with you, there’s a reason so reflect, tighten up and keep going until you get that YES. No just stands for next opportunity! 

SN: To this day I still try on the sneakers app because it forever reminds me that you better get real comfortable “failing” 😇 You have to be willing to keep going even when it seems like all you know is “Your draw was not selected” 🥲😂

I don’t see myself going back to one but there’s nothing wrong with it, I know a lot of folks bringing in 200k+ from working a 9-5 and that’s guaranteed money. title: Today marks two anniversaries and I decided to share some nostalgia.
caption:
Today marks two anniversaries and I decided to share some nostalgia.

My first time doing a public speaking event formally 🎙(5 Years Ago)

My first time opening a business account 💼 

Fast forward 5 years to doing more events than I can count, helping more people than I could have fathomed and having so much fun along the way. 

I traveled with $100 in my account same as when I have 400,000 points 😂 

I was willing to work 70 hours weeks for the few weeks in between semesters just to make a few grand now it takes a lot less time and energy to do the same. I was willing to do 21 credit hours while working, sitting on boards and balancing a social life because I wanted free time for business 😅

DON’T be afraid of the work 🛠 

Anyone who tells your it’s gonna be easy, that you won’t face some burn out or you won’t screw up relationships, finances at some point or question your own why is setting you up to fail. 

Find your life group and edit it as necessary as things change and learn BOUNDARIES (took me a while to get comfortable saying no) 😂 

As Q4 begins set your goals, make sure your habits match them and crush it!!

title: Curaçao Recap 😊
caption:
Curaçao Recap 😊

Went down for a wedding that got cancelled last minute but decided to go enjoy the beach regardless. It’ll be reschedule and I’ll definitely slide back 😇

Found out Curaçao is an amazing Jazz hub and have some awesome annual events (pre-COVID). Plenty of neat places, restaurants, views and beautiful water.

It took me a long time to understand floating because it really requires an aspect of “letting go”. I remember almost drowning as a kid which made me paranoid in water for a long time but you’ve gotta accept things as they are and get on the other side of fear. It’s my favorite thing now because you can lay back and think 💭 in silence since your ears are normally submerged 💧

Marriott has a pretty nice resort with restaurants and music on site plussss there’s a lounge at the airport for my priority pass holders can get some food 🥘 and drinks 🍹 before heading back to the US 😇

Every time I go somewhere new my favorite part is talking to locals about their lives and journey. Each time you step a little farther out you see how vast the 🌎 really is 😍

Magical Tunes 🎼: @petercollinsmusic 
Literally heard this song today, made a fan out of me haha !

title: Went to grab gas while in Tennessee before dinner with my favorite car club and a nice kid rolls up me and asks me about
caption:
Went to grab gas while in Tennessee before dinner with my favorite car club and a nice kid rolls up me and asks me about my car. 

Rather than just let him look I wanted him to experience something. Let him turn it on, showed him around and his face was priceless 😭

He asked me about my Kenya 🇰🇪 bracelet and I made him a deal if he agreed to believe in himself and go after whatever the wildest things he wanted he can have it. Kids should be shown what’s possible and we should be excited to help them along the way! Self talk is huge but we have the ability to speak life into others as well so use your words to uplift and encourage someone else.

Happy Sunday ❤️

title: The different stages of entrepreneurship shown in my half good half bad golfing 😅
caption:
The different stages of entrepreneurship shown in my half good half bad golfing 😅

1. You’ve prepped you’ve tried but you didn’t hit it perfectly...however you hit it straight and you have a straight light to get to so you can take the next shot continued in the right direction. (Incremental progress is still progress and trying is the first step)
2. You hit the shot!..BUT there’s a hole you didn’t prep for. That’s completely okay because now you’ve gotten to a point of understanding how to make the right shot in the right direction and now you’ve just gotta calibrate a little more on where to land. Things will pop out all the time you aren’t expecting but you got to not let it stop you from shooting.
3. You SMASH your goals(maybe you don’t wanna over shoot in golf) but you’ll supersede everything you have been preparing for! 

The ♻️ will continue but you know that through practice, preparation, moving your feet, adjusting your aim and continuously shooting you end up DESTROYING YOUR GOALS. 

Shoutout to @dr.eddielee as always for some great times, knowledge and the push to uphold and upkeep moving the needle amongst brothers ✊🏾😎

title: Was finally able to say I saw my score touch 800 last week (on one of the models lol the rest will catch up in a month o
caption:
Was finally able to say I saw my score touch 800 last week (on one of the models lol the rest will catch up in a month or so) 😇 Anything above a 760 will get you the best rates but now I’ll just work towards an 850. It’s just been an interesting journey learning by doing the wrong things to learn what was right.

To give back I wanted to share what I wish I knew before I started building personal and business credit here are a few things I wish I knew that I hope you all pass along....

1. Prequalify for cards before applying. You can type in amex prequalify or Bank of America prequalify for two examples to get a SOFT inquiry and see what they are likely to approve you for. It’s not a guarantee but it’s a wholeeeee lot better than applying blind and I’ve seen a 90%+ approvals with clients.

2. Trade lines can definitely drastically boost your score BUT there’s nothing like having a string credit file of your own and you can organically do that over 2-4 years easily. (Trade lines for those who don’t know are nothing more than a term used by creditors to describe credit accounts listed on your credit profile.) You can buy trade lines online or from trusted sources. You only want to be on accounts old in age, at 0% utilization and with a nice limit.

3. YOU CAN REVERSE NEGATIVE ITEMS. Don’t feel bad that you have something in collections or missed a payment. Look up what a Goodwill letter is and how to send certified mail to ask nicely to get it off. If it’s a collection barter your way down to pennies on the dollar pay it then send in that receipt along with your dispute to get it removed. If you rack up the debt, take care of it even, if it’s for a lot less you pay in comparison.

Bonus: You don’t have to wait to get invited for a Black Card anymore you can initiate an invitation at www.centurion.com if you are spending 250k-500k on your Amex Platinum Personal or Business

title: I used to wonder if there were ever enough hours in a day but eventually I came to realize that time well spent is more
caption:
I used to wonder if there were ever enough hours in a day but eventually I came to realize that time well spent is more than enough. I chased, conquered and obtained everything I wanted in this first quarter of life but I know more than anything else I’m glad I found such amazing people to spend it with, learn from and pour into. As I reminisce on old times I went through one of my time capsules to find letters from my 9th grade teacher who helped me refind my passion for math, a thank you card from my 10th grade biology teacher who gave me one of my first platforms to do public speaking years ago, kind remarks from the coordinator of a robotics program I did at GSU in 10th grade that made me love the school so much I came back twice, a letter from a patient from my Kroger pharmacy days, a letter from a classmate I got to explore working in hospitals with in Europe, a letter from the mentor that made and invested in me to start a consulting company, a heartfelt note from a intern turned friend and a Christmas Drawing from a pharmacist and friend who passed years ago and even that small note reminds me of our hilarious and real conversations. All that to say I wouldn’t have accomplished a single thing by myself and I’m blessed with an abundance of mentors, friends that sharpen my iron and people I’ve dedicated to mentor and push them same as others have done me. The birthday wishes are all appreciated but I’ll challenge everyone to drop a sentence or two of something you’ve learned through your life no matter how young or old that can help someone else. Can’t wait for what the next 25 years has in store ❤️

title: Happy 29th Anniversary to two of my biggest supporters and inspiration in life ❤️ On the day that represents the beauty
caption: Happy 29th Anniversary to two of my biggest supporters and inspiration in life ❤️ On the day that represents the beauty of the love you all have cherished you all never stop putting service above self. My Uncle Gabriel passed after 12 years in a coma just days ago, and without hesitation, they canceled travel plans to help organize a memorial & support the family. RIP Uncle Gabe🙏🏾Soon as the memorial was over, my Mom was speaking at a conference; they helped organize that was centered around one of the most prominent speakers from Kenya.  After 10 hours of services(foreigners can spend church forever but for great reasons), I got to treat my favorite people to dinner. As I continue to grow, I want to show what’s it’s like for my nephews to see the joy of service and giving. Earlier my other Dad(Uncle Gideon) gifted Anthony & Jordan candy as most great grandparents would, and Anthony gifted him $5. He took the $5 and gave Anthony back a $20(which he was stoked about). We decided to hit an old favorite of the Marietta Diner and had the pleasure of Theresa as a server, who brighten our day, so we tried to return the favor. Theresa joked with Anthony that the $20 was her money(he was still talking about it at dinner so she joked and asked to see it then wrote her name on it and said it must be hers), so when she returned, I told him to gift it to her same as he was gifted. Seeing the way she lit up after was great, but little did she know we went stopping at $20. After continuous tipping after each time she returned at the end of our meal, she cried(then my sister cried cause she’s a big ollllll softie) 😭 It’s beautiful to see how generosity multiplies as it’s given. Giving from a cheerful heart with those who are receptive and appreciative makes it all worth it. We aren't generous for the appreciation but rather to spread the joy and blessings bestowed upon us. A heart of giving has been something passed along from watching my parents; it’s not stopping with Lydia and me. My nephews are my pride & joy, and I want to teach them the same heart of giving(with better boundaries because it took me a while to understand a need of someone is not always your calling lol

title: With the assistance of @nashbiofficial, today was another moment where I got to speak on not only my passion but my hear
caption: With the assistance of @nashbiofficial, today was another moment where I got to speak on not only my passion but my heart. It takes some courage to realize that when you look at life through a lens of growth, you’ll never become your best self. Outside of that, realizing that life is a lot more like playing an instrument than a journey. I am learning to enjoy every moment and choose happiness. My final advice to the students wasn’t to save money or change budgets but to strive to understand themselves and what makes them happy. A lot of changes are happening, and I’m excited to enjoy each and every moment — cheers to another opportunity to share beautiful moments with beautiful people. The end of 2019 has been great in reinforcing what’s important as I embark on the next phase of life. Special thanks to all of my amazing friends and family, both new and old. Let’s make not only this day but every day whatever we want life to be.

title: “Perseverance is the hard work you do after you get tired of doing the hard work you already did.” As 2019 wraps up, I’m
caption: “Perseverance is the hard work you do after you get tired of doing the hard work you already did.” As 2019 wraps up, I’m beyond excited to dive into the next season of life. These are random pieces from Q3 (peep that beautiful golf shot and sunset from Amelia Island 😍) I’m beyond appreciative for all of the friends and family that make it possible not just to survive but to thrive! I wanted to teach and got the chance to do multiple workshops with more and more being scheduled. I wanted direction for what to do next, and I’m lucky enough to have plenty of wise folks in my camp to support and give insight. “If you want to be able to survive the various storms of life, make sure you have planted roots. Oak trees survive while Pine trees and snapped and blown away because of their lack of roots.” I had a decision to make on whether I would stay in Georgia or California, and I’m deciding to stay and cultivate my roots. I’m still pending for a fellowship in California, but I’ve made up my mind. My mentors who have guided me, my friends who walk beside me, and my family, who is always behind me, are all here. I plan to keep cultivating what I have in this city that I love and watch as I do my best to not necessarily change the world but make the impact I was destined to make. Atlanta is home, and if You want to make a difference and make it better, let’s do it together. 😌

title: Prosperity can be defined as a state of flourishing, thriving, good fortune or successful social status. One of my favor
caption: Prosperity can be defined as a state of flourishing, thriving, good fortune or successful social status. One of my favorite quotes from Verna was “I measure prosperity by how the health and safety of our children are and we have some ways to go..” Special shoutout to @civicdinners for providing a free platform to connect with strangers and talk about topics that matter. We talked about what prosperity means to us, what we fundamentally believe perpetuates economic inclusion and how we plan to impact our communities. Sign up for a dinner. Host a dinner. Have a conversation about how to make the world better. You might not agree with someone else but the first step is the conversation! 😎

title: 24 Years later I’m proud to say that I’m far from finished but happy with where I am 😌 I’ve got to watch my nephews get
caption: 24 Years later I’m proud to say that I’m far from finished but happy with where I am 😌 I’ve got to watch my nephews get older, graduate college, travel more than ever, start and grow my full time business, hire someone, fire someone, get awesome interns, help people start over 20 businesses, educate friends, family and strangers on finances, visit my potential graduate school and survive trading $1,027,230.05*transactional amounts not current balance* (plenty of losses and plenty of profits in between it all) in the last year. I never faced so many obstacles and doubts as the past year, but I’m a firm believer that pressure makes diamonds and tough people outlast tough times.(Doesn't make the hard times any easier though) I used to despise failing, but I've come to terms with mistakes being a part of the journey and that they can all be a blessing or a lesson. I’m excited to fail more, learn more from those I encounter and help as many folks as I can along the way. Special thanks to my amazing family, friends, and even the strangers who have poured into my life to help give a new perspective and help make me better mentally, physically, and spiritually. All the investment pitches, meals, reflective conversations, networking events, research, and sleepless nights were far from in vain. I understand that with growth in mind, I’ll never be my best self. However, I’ll never let it stop me from pushing forward through the next challenge and to the next level.

title: “You’re obviously in the wrong place”. Everyone no matter what their professions, goals or dreams has moments of doubt.
caption: “You’re obviously in the wrong place”. Everyone no matter what their professions, goals or dreams has moments of doubt. We are often afraid of failure and it stops us from going out to chase our dreams. Over the past 2 years I’m beyond honored to say I have run across some of the most amazing individuals and lived through my most difficult challenges. When we run across people, sometimes we only share a moment, sometimes a month and for some a lifetime. I am a firm believer that at most, we can ever achieve is our wildest dreams and those you surround yourself with help make it an easier or harder journey. So I challenge everyone to dream bigger, check those you allow to influence you and make life whatever you want because it’s too short to look through a lens of limitation. I have constantly failed along my journey and I look forward to failing more because I have people to help me be better and failure is the predecessor to success. To put it in perspective, I’m afraid of heights and went zip lining through a underground cave partially because I’m a part time adrenaline junkie but also because I don’t let my fear stop me from doing anything I want. Remember that “Ego is the anesthesia that deadens the pain of stupidity” so don’t let it get in the way of saying I need help or have failed. As we all enter a new season I wish everyone success, the willpower to persevere and a little bit of luck!

title: This trip has been extremely eye opening as far as cultural perspectives on masculinity and colorism. “Ser negro es ser
caption: This trip has been extremely eye opening as far as cultural perspectives on masculinity and colorism. “Ser negro es ser pecado” or “To be black is a sin” are words that I would have never expected from a country filled with people of color from Haitian and Dominican descent. All over the world people have been put in the mindset that color is detrimental or contributes towards people’s work ethic or socioeconomic status. Rather than post “poverty porn” I’m posting photos of positive moments with some amazing individuals who I know without a doubt will do their part to make a difference. Yes, the world is filled with poverty, people in situations worse than ourselves and seemingly hopeless situations we can all do our part to learn about ourselves, our communities and how to work as a collective to make socially conscious plans to help the populations of the people. More than anything this trip has added fuel to the flame of philanthropy in my heart and I’ll do what I can now but can’t wait until I can do everything I want to do.
